matches.name: Walmart
matches.description: Dominant low-cost retailer, scale moat, stable category. Less membership-driven loyalty than Costco; lower margin trajectory.

matches.name: Home Depot
matches.description: Category-dominant big-box, scale + brand moat, stable category. Disciplined capital allocation under Frank Blake.

matches.name: TJX Companies
matches.description: Off-price retail, treasure-hunt model, scale buying advantage, consistent operator. Closest peer in 'flywheel-discipline' archetype.

matches.name: BJ's Wholesale Club
matches.description: Direct warehouse-club competitor, similar membership model, smaller scale and weaker brand. Same archetype, smaller magnitude.

matches.name: Dollar General
matches.description: Discount retail, rural footprint moat, stable category. Different customer than Costco but similar 'low-end discipline' archetype.

matches.name: Bed Bath & Beyond
matches.description: Specialty retail with apparent moat that eroded — coupon-trained customer base, no membership lock-in, weak digital. Cautionary parallel.

pullquote_html: Costco's nearest neighbours are the category-dominant compounders of the last cycle. The one cautionary case (BBBY) lacked Costco's membership-locked customer — the feature that converts a strong position into a durable one.
